label: task75-runtime-contract-query
non_goals: No hosted knowledge-base platform, search service, LMS, video host, Q&A service, dashboard, analytics backend, access-control system, or external service is created or contacted.

No source documentation, report, Taskmaster task, session, plan, work-tracking artifact, Serena memory, template, Git state, or external state is mutated beyond requested knowledge-base report artifacts.
No copy-export tree, duplicate documentation store, database, full-text search index service, crawler daemon, scheduler, notification system, or webhook is created.
No reader identity, permission model, audit log, usage analytics, or contribution workflow is inferred beyond existing repo-native evidence.

search_results.summary: This guide explains how to work in this repository now. The system is not just a prompt collection. It is a portable workflow foundation made of Taskmaster tasks, dated sessions, plans, work-tracking folders, guard checks, evidence files, and agent runtime contracts.
search_results.title: Codex Foundation User Guide

search_results.summary: Claude readiness is the first mechanical gate in the Claude runtime adapter. It is a read-only check that decides whether Claude may perform persistent mutations in the project.
search_results.title: Claude Readiness

search_results.summary: Implemented by Taskmaster Task 103 and being live-hardened by Taskmaster Task 105. Task 103 delivered the initial readiness gate, PreToolUse dispatcher, Claude commands, agents, settings, and focused tests. Task 105 validates that contract against the current repository state and
search_results.title: Claude Runtime Contract

search_results.summary: The shared foundation documentation was originally written for Codex and often names Codex tools. Claude must translate those references at call time. Do not edit shared handlers or templates to rename tools.
search_results.title: Claude Tool Mapping

usage_guidance: Use the Markdown runbook for human navigation.
Use the JSON payload for automation, grep-like discovery, or future MCP installer/index work.
Run with --query to generate focused packets for a concept, task, command, or workflow.
Update source documentation at its canonical path; do not edit generated knowledge-base packets as source of truth.
